mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production indicate expanding supply capacity and economic strength, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases month-over-month over 6 months but SPX 60-day returns are negative in >50% of such periods, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25%, it signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25%, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of cases over a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signal synchronized expansion in both supply and labor demand, boosting confidence and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: month-over-month increase in both INDPRO and PAYEMS for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both series rising) produces positive 20-day returns in less than 55% of occurrences over a 5-year period, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, rising consumer price inflation combined with low unemployment pressures the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, raising financing costs and reducing future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If when CPIAUCSL YoY > 2% and UNRATE < 5%, SPX 60-day returns are positive more often than negative (i.e., hit rate >50%) over the walk-forward period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects robust aggregate demand and rising corporate sales, which supports higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If quarterly real GDP growth is positive but SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters over two business cycles, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in nonfarm payrolls month-over-month signals weakening labor demand and deteriorating economic conditions, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines month-over-month but SPX 20-day returns are positive in >60% of such occurrences over any 3-year walk-forward window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1% and consumer price inflation is rising persistently, it signals that monetary policy is becoming overly accommodative relative to growth, creating a risk of future tightening that depresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.2% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If the interaction condition (low rates + rising inflation) is met but SPX 20-day returns are positive in >50% of instances over two years of out-of-sample data, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below the 1,000-thousand-unit threshold signals a contraction in residential investment and weakening consumer confidence, which leads to a near-term drop in equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If housing starts fall below 1,000 but SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of such events over any 5-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.
